rationale: Added the verus! prelude plus the supporting types (Ordering, KeyTrait, VerusClone, StrictlyOrderedVec, sorted) so det.rs can compile standalone. First attempt modeled StrictlyOrderedVec as wrapping a Vec<K> like the source: the proof failed because in Verus, Vec spec-equality is NOT view-based (confirmed with a minimal test). To close the obligation, modeled the struct's inner field as Seq<K> (only the spec representation matters for this determinism check), which gives view-based extensional equality. Proof block guards on the antecedent, then asserts =~= on views, on the inner Seq, and on the struct itself; r1==r2 is trivial for unit. verus exits 0 with 1 verified, 0 errors.
